patterns.title: Resilience — anticipate / respond / recover
patterns.cue: uncertainty · ambiguity · timeline+budget + SH concern
patterns.action: Build resilient approach (not 'eliminate all risk')
patterns.trap: Meeting-only · eliminate uncertainty claim

patterns.title: Compliance / ethics — không exception
patterns.cue: regs · HQ vs local · ethics · audit · specialty law
patterns.action: Validate + escalate; never authorize ethics/regs exceptions
patterns.trap: Local exception / cut compliance for deadline / cover deviation

patterns.title: Compliance audit / catch overlooked regs
patterns.cue: multi regs · process improvement · overlooked requirements
patterns.action: Schedule audit / independent certification to prove standards
patterns.trap: Assume compliant · skip baseline audit

patterns.title: Transparency — tin xấu / bất định
patterns.cue: layoff · incomplete info · rumor · morale crash
patterns.action: Share known facts + facilitate open discussion
patterns.trap: Distract · promise certainty · silence · hide

patterns.title: Coach conflict — không làm hộ
patterns.cue: privately struggling with teammate · interpersonal conflict
patterns.action: Coach conflict-resolution techniques; facilitate
patterns.trap: PM takes sides / punish / ignore / solve for them

patterns.title: Competency gap → develop
patterns.cue: interns · skill gap · training needs · onboarding
patterns.action: Gap analysis + develop/remediate (HR/mentor) — don't blame
patterns.trap: Fire / blame / ignore capability gap

patterns.title: Knowledge transfer — outgoing / incoming
patterns.cue: turnover · transition · strict domain · KT sessions
patterns.action: Prioritize immediate knowledge transfer between people
patterns.trap: Delay KT · assume documentation enough under pressure

patterns.title: OPA / lessons → actionable update
patterns.cue: estimation bias · vague policy · many changes delivered · lessons
patterns.action: Update OPA / templates / rewrite policy — actionable
patterns.trap: Archive-only / refer historical / interview without change

patterns.title: Risk cadence — register & response
patterns.cue: risk materialized · new law · risk in reports · reserve
patterns.action: Implement planned response · identify/assess · include in reports
patterns.trap: Invent new ritual · ignore register · lessons instead of act

patterns.title: Change control / ICC
patterns.cue: out of baseline · unauthorized work · new feature request · CCB
patterns.action: Stop unauthorized if needed → impact → formal CR/CCB
patterns.trap: Absorb scope because 'value' · silent add · informal list

patterns.title: Verify scope / acceptance trước 'done'
patterns.cue: sponsor confirm done · acceptance criteria · close check
patterns.action: Review scope & requirements / acceptance before claim success
patterns.trap: Declare done from timeline or team feeling alone

patterns.title: Quality — sampling / certification evidence
patterns.cue: mass production · quality supervisor · prove standards
patterns.action: Statistical sampling · independent certification test
patterns.trap: Inspect 100% when sampling fits · skip proof of quality

patterns.title: Agile MVP / backlog / iteration protect
patterns.cue: MVP · mid-iteration · PO absent · backlog · sprint review
patterns.action: PO owns backlog · ship MVP · protect iteration · workshop AC
patterns.trap: Team absorbs mid-sprint · wait perfect · skip PO

patterns.title: Engagement plan — theo chiến lược SH
patterns.cue: resistant stakeholder · conflict interests · power/interest
patterns.action: Consult engagement plan; engage per documented strategy
patterns.trap: Freestyle escalate / ignore minority SH

patterns.title: Adapt communications + culture
patterns.cue: preferred channel · multicultural · virtual · language
patterns.action: Adapt methods to prefs; culturally responsive protocols
patterns.trap: One-size email blast · ignore time zone/culture

patterns.title: Org culture change — targeted, not slogans
patterns.cue: hierarchy→agile · restructuring · vision/alignment drift
patterns.action: Targeted change activities · clear vision with sponsor/team
patterns.trap: Announce values only · ignore process/structure redesign

patterns.title: Own mistake — acknowledge + apologize
patterns.cue: wrong recipient email · unintended consequence · visible error
patterns.action: Acknowledge the mistake and apologize for consequences
patterns.trap: Hide · blame tool · over-explain without owning

patterns.title: Charter — authorize & align
patterns.cue: global HQ ops · need formal start · expectations
patterns.action: Create project charter to authorize and align
patterns.trap: Start work without charter · informal kickoff only

patterns.title: Translate plan → actionable controls
patterns.cue: plan too abstract · audits/checklists
patterns.action: Iterative translations · audits/checklists from the plan
patterns.trap: Leave plan on shelf · verbal alignment only

patterns.title: Benefits / value metrics (not vanity)
patterns.cue: customer satisfaction · Kano · % value · long-term insight
patterns.action: Kano/surveys/benchmarking · measure value achieved · SH behavior insight
patterns.trap: Vanity SPI-only · ignore outcome metrics

patterns.title: Money — forecast / EVM / contract
patterns.cue: budget forward · CPI/SPI · EAC · vendor · unpaid
patterns.action: Update forecasts · EVM act · close contracts admin-complete
patterns.trap: Freeze BAC forever · overtime-only · skip admin close

patterns.title: Governance / roles / RAM / steering
patterns.cue: steering review · RACI/RAM · decision authority · unclear duty
patterns.action: Clarify roles (RAM) · prepare governance evidence · decision log
patterns.trap: Freestyle without authority map · skip steering prep

patterns.title: Tailor / hybrid — vừa đủ nghi thức
patterns.cue: small low-risk · mixed certainty · predictive+agile
patterns.action: Tailor weight · hybrid OK · adaptive estimates where uncertain
patterns.trap: Copy megaproject ceremony · or zero governance

patterns.title: Team stage / EI / work environment
patterns.cue: Tuckman · storming · shouting · noisy workspace · ownership
patterns.action: Map stage · EI/self-reg · PM owns environment · facilitate ownership
patterns.trap: Micromanage · escalate people issues early · ignore culture

patterns.title: Identify / re-analyze stakeholders
patterns.cue: new SH · stale distribution · who needs info now
patterns.action: Stakeholder analysis / update register before tweaking list alone
patterns.trap: Edit distribution list by gut without analysis

patterns.title: Compliance research before proceed
patterns.cue: new requirement after charter · specialty regs · ambiguity
patterns.action: Research compliance requirement (BA) before authorizing work
patterns.trap: Start anyway · assume sponsor already cleared regs

rows.correctLabel: C. Account for time zones, tool availability, and potential constraints when planning the meeting.

rows.correctLabel: D. Use a predictive approach with fixed deployment sequences and incorporate continuous improvement cycles after each wave.

rows.correctLabel: D. Apply statistical sampling to inspect a representative subset of products and use the results to infer overall quality levels.

rows.correctLabel: B. Hold regular product backlog refinement sessions to create small, clear, testable items that are ready for sprint planning.

rows.correctLabel: D. Meet with the stakeholders to gather and integrate their feedback into the iteration backlog decisions.

rows.correctLabel: C. Facilitated a formal handover of the product to the appropriate support team before closing the project.

rows.correctLabel: B. Evaluate the cost of quality and sustainability tradeoffs and request a corresponding change to the quality management plan if warranted.

rows.stem: The actions of a new project will be bound by the organization’s documented policies, standards, and procedures. The project manager needs to make these artifacts easily accessible for the team and to
rows.correctLabel: C. Organize the documents on a central website that individual team members can access as often as needed.

rows.stem: At project initiation, a vendor provided a quote for the project's required services totaling US$100,000. As the project progressed, evolving requirements led to a revised statement of work and procur

rows.stem: A new project manager has taken over an ongoing project that seems to be on track with budget and schedule, based on progress reports. A key supplier suddenly informs the project manager that they can
rows.correctLabel: A. Conduct an impact analysis on schedule, budget, and quality to understand the full implications.

rows.stem: A new product development project has been launched. The project sponsor is confident that the project will be successful. However, key investors are less confident and require more information about
rows.correctLabel: A. Present detailed market analysis, business plan, and risk management strategies to investors.

rows.stem: The project manager demonstrates a prototype to the customer halfway through a system development project. The customer notes that several key features are missing and escalates the issue to key stake
rows.correctLabel: D. Validate whether the missing features are included in the approved scope baseline and WBS deliverables.
